creator: jguojun@sbcglobal.net
text:
One issue is that CTRL-ALTR-F# will hangs the XWindow (not blank the screen but frozen).

kill -9 "Xorg pid" cannot bring screen to terminal mode (X Window is frozen and hanging forever).

I am not sure if this is the same symptom you mentioned earlier.


________________________________
 From: Jin Guojun <jguojun@sbcglobal.net>
To: Jean-Sébastien Pédron <dumbbell@FreeBSD.org>; "bug-followup@FreeBSD.org" <bug-followup@FreeBSD.org> 
Sent: Friday, February 14, 2014 12:38 PM
Subject: Re: ports/185182: x11/xorg of 10-RC2 has blank screen on APU-3400 system
 


root@FB10a:~ # cat /etc/rc.conf 
hostname="FB10a"
ifconfig_alc0="DHCP"
sshd_enable="YES"
moused_enable="YES"
ntpd_enable="YES"
powerd_enable="YES"
# Set dumpdev to "AUTO" to enable crash dumps, "NO" to disable
dumpdev="AUTO"
hald_enable=YES


BTW, the radeon driver seems working now after Vesa driver installed.


________________________________
 From: Jean-Sébastien Pédron <dumbbell@FreeBSD.org>
To: Jin Guojun <jguojun@sbcglobal.net>; "bug-followup@FreeBSD.org" <bug-followup@FreeBSD.org> 
Sent: Thursday, February 13, 2014 2:05 AM
Subject: Re: ports/185182: x11/xorg of 10-RC2 has blank screen on APU-3400 system
 

On 13.02.2014 07:45, Jin Guojun wrote:
> Moused is running because USB mouse works fine.

moused(8) is start automatically by devd(8) when a USB mouse is plugged
in. This is not the case for a PS/2 mouse: you have to specify
"moused_enable=YES" in your /etc/rc.conf.

-- 
Jean-Sébastien Pédron
